title: 10 best herbal tinctures for coughing fits
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for coughing fits are liquid extracts of plants that help alleviate persistent and severe coughing, often caused by respiratory infections, allergies, or irritants. These tinctures are effective due to their concentrated bioactive compounds that soothe the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for coughing fits include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Verbascum thapsus, Sambucus nigra, Hydrastis canadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, coughing fits can lead to sleep deprivation, fatigue, and impaired daily functioning, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with coughing fits because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes in the throat. This natural ingredient reduces inflammation and relaxes the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs. Additionally, herbal licorice tinctures often combine with other soothing herbs like thyme, slippery elm, and marshmallow root, which further amplify their calming effects on the respiratory system. As a result, licorice tinctures have been traditionally used to ease symptoms of bronchitis, pneumonia, and other respiratory infections that cause persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughs and congestion.

Sothing demulcent properties: The saponins present in licorice root create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing and calming irritated tissues, thereby reducing coughing fits and alleviating discomfort.
Expectorant properties: Licorice tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion, which can contribute to persistent coughs.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Liquiritin: A phenolic compound, liquiritin has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.

Licodione: A phenolic compound, licodione has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their ability to help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties, which help to relieve coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable source in late summer or early fall.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or tool.
Steep the chopped roots in a neutral solvent such as ethanol or glycerin at a ratio of 1:2 for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the solvent from the roots through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before use allowing the flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The tincture contains compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and anthocyanins that help soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, elder tincture's expectorant properties loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief from chronic coughing fits, helping to ease the discomfort and promote a peaceful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Elder tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm, which can help alleviate symptoms of a persistent cough.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The bioactive compounds in elder tincture have been shown to possess antiviral and antibacterial activities, helping to combat infections that may be contributing to the coughing fit and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture contains flavonoids and other polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and throat, making it less uncomfortable to cough and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Sambunigrin: This glycoside has been found to have a soothing effect on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and helping to calm coughing fits.

Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, vitamin C in Sambucus nigra helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity of coughing fits by inhibiting the replication of respiratory viruses and reducing inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help soothe coughs.
Buds: They are used because they contain similar bioactive compounds to the flowers, which help alleviate coughs and respiratory issues.
Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation associated with coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer when they are in full bloom to ensure optimal potency.
Clean the freshly harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine 1 part sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, calming the mucous membranes and relieving congestion. Additionally, thyme's natural antibacterial properties can help combat infections that may be contributing to the cough. By using a herbal thyme tincture, individuals can experience quick and effective relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Thyme tincture's ability to reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits helps alleviate respiratory discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help soothe and calm irritated respiratory mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with coughs.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Thyme tincture's antimicrobial agents have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria that can cause respiratory infections, helping to combat the underlying cause of the cough.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a monoterpenic phenol that exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways and alleviate coughing fits.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic monoterpene with antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the severity of coughing fits by targeting and eliminating the underlying infection or inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial and expectorant properties, helping to ease coughs and relieve congestion.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used for their calming effects and to help soothe a sore throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughing fits.
Buds: Thyme buds are used for their concentrated thymol content and to provide an intense, quick-acting effect in relieving coughs, as they are often more potent than leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning or early afternoon.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags or trays for several days.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris plant material with a carrier solvent such as ethanol 80% 1 part thymus vulgaris to 2 parts solvent in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every few days to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties. The menthol content in peppermint helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and throat. This relief can provide instant comfort for those suffering from persistent coughs. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the respiratory tract can also help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, further alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thus relieving congestion and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the throat and airways, which can help soothe irritated tissues and alleviate coughing spasms.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The menthol content in peppermint tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities, helping to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: A monoterpenoid compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve coughing by thinning mucus and promoting expectoration.
Menthone: A monoterpenoid compound that acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax the airways and alleviate coughing by making breathing easier.
Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve coughing by thinning mucus and promoting expectoration, also exhibiting expectorant and bronchodilatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural expectorant properties that help relieve coughs and congestion.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain a high amount of menthol and other volatile oils, making them a popular choice for tinctures aimed at soothing coughs and easing respiratory issues.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a higher concentration of menthone and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Chop the herbs into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped herbs with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with coughing fits because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues. The tannins in mullein also work to reduce inflammation and excess mucus production, making it easier to expel congestion from the lungs. As a natural expectorant, mullein tinctures can loosen and clear phlegm from the airways, providing fast relief from persistent coughing fits. By addressing the underlying causes of coughing, mullein tinctures offer a safe and effective way to manage respiratory symptoms and promote overall lung health.
remedy_properties: Soothing demulcent properties: Mullein's mucilages coat and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and soothing harsh coughs.
Expectorant properties: Mullein helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, alleviating coughing fits caused by inflammation and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, which can become irritated and inflamed during a coughing fit.
Mucilages: As a thick, protective substance, mucilages help to calm and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that can trigger coughing fits.

Flavonoids (including Verbascoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways while also promoting the removal of mucus, which can help to alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties, which soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
Stems: The stems of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections that can cause coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least two weeks or until it reaches 10 moisture content.
Grind the dried verbascum thapsus plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of the powdered plant material with 500 milliliters of 95 ethanol in a glass jar and let it sit for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony tinctures helps with coughing fits because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The tincture's active ingredients, such as baicalin and paeoniflorin, work to reduce inflammation and calm irritation, providing relief from persistent coughs and congestion. As a natural expectorant, peony tincture also helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chinese Peony's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, alleviating coughing fits caused by inflammation.
Expectorant property: The tincture's expectorant properties loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving persistent coughs.
Antispasmodic property: Chinese Peony's antispasmodic compounds help relax the muscles in the airways and lungs, reducing spasms that can trigger or worsen coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, alleviating coughing fits.

Triterpenoids (including paeoniflorin): Paeoniflorin has been shown to have sedative and analgesic effects, which can help calm the nervous system and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Phenolic acids (including paeonilactone A): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Paeonia lactiflora contain paeonol, an anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce coughing and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Paeonia lactiflora are rich in flavonoids and glycosides, which may help soothe a sore throat and calm coughing.
Barks: The barks of Paeonia lactiflora may contain phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh paeonia lactiflora flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Chop 500g of the gathered plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped plant material in 2 liters of 25% ethanol for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place and allow it to mature for 2-3 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs and bronchial tubes. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing fits. As a result, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide natural and effective relief from persistent and annoying coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: Dandelion tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can help alleviate persistent coughing fits.
Expectorant: The tannins present in dandelion tincture help loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which can contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin that helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Polysaccharides: Complex carbohydrates that stimulate the immune system, enhance mucus clearance, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties to combat coughing and respiratory issues.
Inulin: A fructan that exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break down mucus and reduce coughing fits by facilitating easy expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help alleviate coughs.
Roots: Utilized for their rich source of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe coughs.
Flowers: Employed for their high concentration of volatile oils and flavonoids, which have expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and provide relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves on a sunny day.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it occasionally.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its soothing properties, which calm the irritations in the throat and lungs. The herbal extract contains citral and limonene, compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to reduce inflammation and combat infection. Additionally, lemon verbena's expectorant qualities loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel, providing quick relief from coughing fits. As a result, herbal lemon verbena tinctures offer a natural and effective way to alleviate persistent and stubborn coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive (cough-suppressant): Lemon verbena's active compounds, such as citral and citronellal, have been shown to reduce coughing fits by soothing the respiratory tract and temporarily suppressing the cough reflex.
Anti-inflammatory: The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, which can contribute to coughing fits caused by irritants or infections.
Expectorant: Lemon verbena's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Verbenalin: A phenanthraquinone derivative that acts as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughing fits by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.
Verbascoside: A phenylethanoid glycoside that possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Verbenone: A monoterpene that exhibits expectorant and bronchodilatory effects, helping to ease coughing fits by relaxing the airway muscles and facilitating the expulsion of mucus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe coughs and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures because they contain a significant amount of volatile oils and other compounds that help to relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing coughing fits.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested verbena officinalis in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part dried verbena officinalis with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways. The antispasmodic effects of fennel also help to relax and soothe the respiratory muscles, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing episodes. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, further alleviating symptoms of a cough. Overall, herbal fennel tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for managing coughing fits and promoting respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing congestion and relieving coughs.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic effects help relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing spasms and inflammation that can exacerbate coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which can contribute to coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole acts as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughs by increasing the production of mucus and promoting its expulsion from the respiratory tract.
Fenchone: Fenchone possesses anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and make breathing easier, thereby alleviating coughing fits.

Eugenol: Eugenol exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to combat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, ultimately contributing to relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are the most commonly used part for making tinctures to relieve coughing fits due to their high concentration of carvone and limonene, which have expectorant properties.

Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are used to make tinctures for coughing fits because they contain a rich source of volatile oils, including anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: Foeniculum vulgare roots are another key part used to make tinctures, as they contain a significant amount of coumarin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves on a dry sunny day.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine 100 grams of chopped foeniculum vulgare with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, native to the Great Plains region, contains compounds that soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs. By reducing swelling and fighting off infections, Kansas coneflower tinctures can help alleviate the severity of coughing fits, providing relief from congestion and discomfort. This natural remedy can be especially effective for those suffering from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or asthma, where conventional medications may not provide adequate relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.

Antiviral properties: The tincture contains antiviral compounds like echinacein, which can help combat viral infections that cause respiratory issues and coughing fits by inhibiting the replication of viruses and boosting the immune system's response.

Mucolytic properties: Kansas coneflower's saponins break down excess mucus in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the severity of coughing fits associated with respiratory infections like bronchitis and pneumonia.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and frequency of coughing fits by soothing the respiratory tract and modulating the immune response.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as 11α-hydroxy-9β,13β-dihydroxy-3-oxo-9α,11α-eudesman-12,15-dienoic acid, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) activities, which help to alleviate coughing fits.

Alkamides: Alkamides, such as isobutylamides and isovalerylamides, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic (muscle-relaxing) properties, which help to reduce coughing fits by relaxing the smooth muscles in the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high concentrations of echinacoside and alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the respiratory tract.
Roots: They also contain echinulin, a glycoside that has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections causing the cough.
Roots: The root system is rich in other bioactive compounds that contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects, making it a popular choice for cough remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia at peak potency and store them in a cool dark place.
Combine the dried roots with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the echinacea tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its unique properties. The extract contains a compound called calamus, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant qualities that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. Additionally, the tincture's ability to relax the airways and reduce inflammation makes it an effective remedy for relieving persistent coughs and congestion, providing quick and lasting relief from even the most stubborn coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Ginkgo biloba tincture helps to suppress or relieve coughs by reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat and airways.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs and respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Ginkgo biloba's saponins help to loosen and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to cough up phlegm and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and bronchitis, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat and airways, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems from reputable sources.
Combine one part ginkgo biloba material with three parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, using one teaspoon (5ml) as needed for cough relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dogwood tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system. The herbal extract contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier for dogs to breathe and expel mucus. As a result, dogwood tincture can effectively alleviate coughing fits caused by allergies, bronchitis, or other respiratory issues, providing quick relief and comfort for your furry friend.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Dogwood tincture helps to suppress coughs by reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing fits.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in dogwood tincture help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier for the body to expel coughed-up phlegm and reducing coughing spasms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dogwood tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and respiratory tract, which can exacerbate coughing fits and make them more frequent and intense.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dogwood plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm coughs by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Barks: The barks of the dogwood plant contain alkaloids that help reduce bronchial spasms and ease congestion, making it effective for relieving coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried cephalanthus occidentalis leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Crush the dried plant material into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Combine the powdered plant material with 100 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage for at least four hours.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking the jar daily during this time to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the bottle with date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs. The antitussive compounds found in lavender also help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughing. Additionally, lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can often trigger or worsen coughing fits. By using English lavender tinctures, individuals may experience relief from persistent and annoying coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Lavender tincture helps to suppress or reduce the frequency and intensity of coughs by calming the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of lavender tincture help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing fits.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lavender's calming effects can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related coughing, allowing for more restful sleep and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing fits.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain camphor, a natural decongestant that helps to loosen mucus and ease congestion caused by coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers from a reputable source and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine 250 grams of the powdered lavender with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all material is fully covered by the liquid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the mixture once daily to allow for proper infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the clear liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, store in the refrigerator and use within 2 years, diluting as needed for oral consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural antitussive properties, which soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The herb's essential oils, including camphor and thujone, work together to reduce inflammation and ease spasms in the airways, providing relief from persistent and chronic coughs. Additionally, hyssop's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in coughing fits. As a result, hyssop tinctures have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and whooping cough.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up congestion and relieving symptoms of chronic bronchitis and pneumonia.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can exacerbate coughing fits and make breathing more difficult, allowing for smoother air passage and relief from discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: Hyssop tincture contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause respiratory infections, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae, reducing the severity and duration of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which help to soothe and calm respiratory tract irritations, making them effective for cough relief.

Leaves: Hyssop leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and congestion, due to their antimicrobial properties and ability to reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the herb into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to release its essential oils.
Combine 100g of chopped hyssopus officinalis with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow tinctures helps with coughing fits because it soothes and calms the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion. The herbal extract contains a gel-like substance that forms a protective film over these areas, providing instant relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, mallow's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for easier breathing and a more effective clearance of excess mucus, ultimately easing coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mallow tincture contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, helping to calm persistent coughing and reduce inflammation.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in mallow tincture act as expectorants, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from coughs and congestion.

Antioxidant activity: Mallow tincture contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to respiratory issues such as chronic coughing and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and calm irritated throats, making them effective for treating coughing fits.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, they help reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried malva sylvestris flowers and leaves in quantities of one cup.
Chop the plant material into smaller pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering it completely.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural soothing properties. The apigenin in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can exacerbate coughing. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the throat, making it less irritating and discomforting. When taken as a tincture, the active compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from coughs and congestion. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective way to calm the mind and body, easing coughing fits and promoting peaceful rest.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's apigenin and bisabolol content help to calm coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe.

Antihistamine-like effects: The flavonoids and terpenoids in chamomile tinctures have a natural antihistamine effect, which helps to reduce histamine production and alleviate symptoms of allergic coughing fits, such as itching and congestion.

Anti-spasmodic properties: Chamomile's essential oils, particularly bisabolol and apigenin, help to relax the smooth muscle in the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making it effective in reducing the severity of coughing fits.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain a high amount of azulene, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Clean and chop the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine 250-500 grams of chopped matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it daily to allow the herbs to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and label the tincture with ingredients dosage and preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. The active compound gingerol has expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. Additionally, ginger's natural warmth can help to ease a sore throat and calm coughing spasms, providing fast and effective relief from annoying coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can contribute to coughing fits by reducing swelling and irritation.
Expectorant and mucolytic activity: Ginger's expectorant and mucolytic properties help to thin out mucus and make it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion and coughing.

Pain-relieving and anti-spasmodic effects: Ginger's pain-relieving and anti-spasmodic properties can help to alleviate the discomfort and spasms associated with coughing fits, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughs.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a popular choice for making ginger tincture because it contains high levels of gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve coughs and congestion.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to make tinctures that target respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root of high-quality and potency from local farmers or your own garden.
Cut the harvested ginger roots into small pieces to facilitate drying and extraction.
Dry the cut ginger pieces in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 7 days or until crisp and brittle.
Combine 250g of dried ginger with 750ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with coughing fits because they contain soothing compounds that calm and relax the respiratory tract. The gentle, sweet properties of beach rose work to ease congestion and reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing spells. Additionally, beach rose has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat the underlying causes of coughing fits, such as colds and allergies, allowing for a more rapid recovery.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Beach rose tincture contains compounds that soothe and calm coughs, providing relief from irritant-induced or inflammatory coughs.
Expectorant properties: The tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Beach rose's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate coughing fits triggered by allergic reactions or viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated respiratory tissues.
Seeds: Containing expectorant properties, they help loosen and expel mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and hacking coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosa x rugosa flowers and leaves in the late morning for optimal potency.
Combine the 100g of dried plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Allow the tincture to age for an additional 2-4 weeks before filtering again and transferring to smaller bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The silymarin in milk thistle has been shown to soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, the expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel. As a result, milk thistle tinctures can provide relief from persistent and stubborn coughing fits, helping to calm the respiratory tract and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk Thistle's silymarin compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle tincture have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion.

Volatile oils: Milk Thistle's volatile oils contain terpenes that have a soothing effect on the respiratory system, which can help calm coughing spasms and provide relief from irritations in the throat.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in silymarin, a bioflavonoid complex that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making them an effective ingredient for calming persistent coughs.

Roots: Containing a unique combination of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of milk thistle have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, including chronic bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers in their flowering stage.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for at least two weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 40% ethanol or vodka to create a tincture mixture at a ratio of one part herb to four parts liquid.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for six weeks shaking daily then strain and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways. The active compounds in sweet cicely, such as polyacetylenes and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. Additionally, sweet cicely's natural antiseptic properties help combat infections that can contribute to persistent coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Sweet Cicely tincture helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of coughs and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe and easing the discomfort associated with coughing fits.
Antitussive Properties: Sweet Cicely contains antitussive agents that directly calm the cough reflex, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet cicely roots have a calming effect on the respiratory system, making them effective in soothing and relieving coughs.
Fruits: The fruits of the sweet cicely plant contain essential oils that help to break up phlegm and mucus, providing relief from stubborn coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest myrrhis odorata leaves at dawn when they are most potent.
Chop the myrrhis odorata leaves into small pieces to increase surface area.
Mix chopped myrrhis odorata with high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for four weeks to allow extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters after four weeks.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal tinctures are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal tinctures like thyme, eucalyptus, and wild cherry bark can help alleviate symptoms of congestion, wheezing, and tightness in the chest. They also reduce inflammation and relax airways, making it easier to breathe and manage coughs.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with coughing fits is often a blend of Thyme, Echinacea, and Licorice Root. Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections, while Echinacea's immune-boosting effects reduce inflammation and promote recovery. Licorice Root soothes the throat and calms the coughing reflex. Taking 20-30 drops of each tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, can provide relief from persistent coughing and promote overall respiratory health. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with coughing fits because it contains licorice root's anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated throats and mucous membranes. The tannins and flavonoids in licorice root work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, providing quick relief from coughing. Additionally, glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice root, has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving congestion and coughing fits associated with respiratory conditions.
Flavonoids (such as isoliquiritigenin and liquiritigenin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe irritated mucous membranes and alleviate coughing fits.
Coumarins (such as scopoletin): They exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help thin mucus, reduce inflammation, and facilitate cough relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help soothe a cough.
Leaves: The leaves are occasionally used, but their glycoside content, although less than the roots, still contributes to their cough-relieving properties.
Barks: The barks are less commonly used, but their glycoside content can still provide relief for coughing fits, although their potency may be lower than that of the roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 kg of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces and weigh 250g using a digital scale for precise measurement.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a large glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its potent immunomodulatory properties, which enable the body to fight off underlying infections that cause coughing. It contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives that help stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and soothe the respiratory tract. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold and flu, such as congestion, sore throats, and persistent coughing, making it a popular natural remedy for cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate coughing fits.
Caffeic Acid Derivatives: They possess antimicrobial properties that help combat infections causing coughing, while also reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory system.

Cichoric Acid: This compound has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help alleviate coughing fits by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response to respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for tinctures due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Leaves: Utilized in tinctures for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory system.

Flowers: Employed in cough remedies due to their high concentration of flavonoids and caffeic acid, which possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the morning to ensure peak potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks to remove moisture content.
Crush 1 part of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into fine particles using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 part of crushed plant material in 5 parts of 80 proof vodka for 2 to 6 weeks in a glass jar.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its decongestant and expectorant properties. The active compounds, such as eucalyptol and camphor, help to thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. This can provide quick relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, the menthol-like aroma of eucalyptus globulus can help to open up air passages, allowing for easier breathing and reduced coughing fits. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe coughs and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (C10H18O): This constituent acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing fits.
Cineole (C10H18O): Similar to eucalyptol, cineole also has expectorant properties, which help to break down and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from coughing and congestion.
Rimanscin (C15H26O): As a bronchodilator, rimanscin helps to relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part due to their high oil content and potent expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce congestion and soothe irritated throats.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat infection and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 part of fresh roots at dawn or late afternoon.
Chop the eucalyptus globulus leaves into small pieces and combine with the chopped roots in a glass jar.
Pour 1 part of 80 proof vodka and 2 parts of 80 proof glycerin over the eucalyptus globulus mixture in the jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the eucalyptus globulus tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its antitussive properties, which work to calm and soothe the respiratory system. The tincture contains thymol, a compound that has natural expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. This can provide relief from persistent coughing, especially at night, and help to reduce the severity of coughing fits. Additionally, thymus vulgaris tinctures may also help to reduce congestion and promote a healthy cough, ultimately providing a natural way to manage coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits by soothing and calming the irritated area.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol possesses antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections causing coughs, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate congestion, reduce inflammation, and soothe the respiratory system, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound with expectorant properties that help relieve coughs.
Flowers: Utilized for their antitussive properties, which help to soothe and calm coughs.
Stems: Employed for their ability to reduce inflammation and act as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a glass jar.
Pour the vodka over the plant material to cover it completely in the jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with coughing fits because they possess natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to soothe the respiratory tract and calm the cough reflex. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, ginger tinctures provide quick relief from coughing fits, making them an effective natural remedy for colds, flu, and bronchitis. The warming properties of ginger also help to ease congestion and promote a more restful sleep.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These constituents help with coughing fits by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which reduce inflammation in the airways and promote the removal of mucus.
Shogaols: They aid in alleviating coughing fits by possessing analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract.

Zingiberene: This constituent helps to relieve coughing fits by exhibiting bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax and widen airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing episodes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving coughs and congestion.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also a common source for tinctures, as they contain similar compounds to the rhizomes, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract.

Leaves: Although less commonly used than the rhizomes or roots, some herbal practitioners use the leaves of Zingiber officinale in tinctures, as they may contain smaller amounts of the beneficial compounds and can be used to provide a milder, more soothing effect on the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh root material in the early morning when the plant is at its peak potency level.
Chop 1 pound of the harvested root material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 pound of the chopped root material with 5 cups of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container to remove the root material residue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tinctures helps with coughing fits because it has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and allowing for easier breathing. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for coughs and colds. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus contains mucilage, a natural demulcent that protects and calms the mucous membranes, providing relief from coughing and promoting a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been found to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby alleviating coughing fits.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Verbascum thapsus possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and relax the muscles involved in coughing.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, including sesquiterpenes, contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects of Verbascum thapsus, thereby reducing the severity of coughing fits caused by respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are rich in mucilages, which provide soothing and calming effects to the respiratory system, making them a popular choice for cough remedies.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used to make tinctures that can help to calm and soothe the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and relieving coughing fits.

Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures that can help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and relieving coughing fits due to their mucilaginous properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh verbascum thapsus flowers at dawn when they are fully open and dry them thoroughly.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the chopped flowers with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every few days to extract the essence.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with coughing fits because they contain bioflavonoids and other compounds that have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. The tincture's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to expel. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as the common cold and flu.
remedy_constituents: Volatile oils: These oils have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus reducing coughing fits.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, which in turn can alleviate coughing fits.
Anthocyanins: These pigments have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the relief of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are used to make tinctures for coughing fits because they contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used to make tinctures for coughing fits because they contain compounds that help soothe and calm the respiratory system.

Fruits (Elderberries): Sambucus nigra fruits (Elderberries) are used to make tinctures for coughing fits because they contain antiviral compounds that help combat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 500 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 1000 milliliters of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days to extract the active ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract. The tannins and alkaloids present in the plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, soothing irritated airways and reducing coughing spasms. By reducing inflammation and promoting mucus clearance, Hydrastis canadensis tinctures can provide relief from persistent coughs and congestion, making it a natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its antitussive properties help calm the coughing reflex.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and lungs, relieving coughing fits.
Glycosides: They have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes and reduce coughing.
Tannins: They act as astringents, helping to dry up excess mucus and reduce coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to soothe coughs.
Leaves: Contain hydrastine, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and congestion.
Barks: Also contain berberine and hydrastine, making them a valuable addition to cough remedies due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hydrastis canadensis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity.
Chop 1 part of the roots into small pieces and mix with 2 parts of vodka 80 proof in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years in a glass dropper bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its soothing and calming properties. The essential oils present in the tincture, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to ease respiratory congestion and reduce the severity of coughing fits. Additionally, the tincture's relaxing properties can help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate coughing. This can lead to a decrease in the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce inflammation and calm the nervous system, thereby easing coughing fits.
Linalyl acetate: Acts as a natural antispasmodic, helping to relax the muscles in the throat and lungs and alleviate coughing spasms.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their calming effects and ability to soothe respiratory issues.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for coughing fits because they contain lavender oil, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to relieve congestion and coughs.
Buds: They are used to make tinctures for coughing fits due to their high content of lavender oil, which helps to calm the respiratory system and reduce coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the harvested lavandula angustifolia flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, allowing for easier breathing. The menthol content in peppermint oil, a key component of mentha x piperita, provides a cooling sensation that soothes the throat and calms coughing spasms. Additionally, mentha x piperita's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the throat, reducing coughing fits and providing relief from respiratory discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
Menthone: It has a soothing effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to coughing fits.
Rosmarinic Acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the severity of coughing fits by calming the airways and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides a cooling and soothing effect on the respiratory system.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are another primary part used for tinctures as they contain a high amount of menthone, which has expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used for making tinctures, particularly for their high menthol content, which aids in reducing inflammation and providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh herb and chop it into small pieces to release its oils and flavors.
Combine the chopped herb with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to a glass dropper bottle and label it for future use.
